name: Doro Wat (Ethiopian Stew)
recipeIngredient: 2 large cloves garlic, minced (or 2 tsp garlic powder)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large stew pot over medium heat.
2. Add onions and garlic; sauté for about 5 minutes until golden brown.
3. Stir in berbere spice mix, then add tomato paste. Cook, stirring occasionally, for approximately 15 minutes to allow flavors to develop.
4. Gently fold in chicken pieces, coating them well with the sauce.
5. Continue simmering, adding water as needed to maintain a thick soup consistency. Simmer until the chicken is nearly cooked through (about 20 minutes).
6. Add the hard boiled eggs and continue cooking until the chicken is fully tender.
7. Let the oil rise to the top of the pot; adjust seasoning with black pepper if desired, then remove from heat.
8. Serve over injera (flat bread) or pita/bread tortillas.
Makes 8 to 10 servings.
